g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain logistic regression input features and feature matrices with an example')
g.text: Each row is a sample; each column is a feature. Shape: (n_samples, n_features)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How are logistic regression weights and bias initialized, and why does it matter?')
g.text: w = zeros(n_features), b = 0. These are what the model learns.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain the linear combination z = Xw + b in logistic regression with an example')
g.text: Step 3 — Linear combination: z = Xw + b
Dot product of features and weights, plus bias. z can be any real number.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the sigmoid function work and why is it used in logistic regression?')
g.text: Squashes z into (0, 1) — interpreted as probability of class 1.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain binary cross-entropy loss in logistic regression with intuition')
sendPrompt('How do we compute gradients in logistic regression — derive dL/dw and dL/db')
sendPrompt('Explain gradient descent weight update in logistic regression and the role of learning rate')
g.text: w = w − α·(∂L/∂w) b = b − α·(∂L/∂b) α = learning rate

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does logistic regression make final predictions using a decision threshold?')
